### Post by cedron on 2007-04-30
I have a Dell Latitude D520 Laptop and am having problems with a slow touchpad. When I try to edit the settings in system->preferences->mouse, nothing happens to the touchpads responsivity. Any ideas of what to do?


Here is my xorg.conf:


# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.

### Post by jackmc on 2007-04-30
Try gsynaptics or qsynaptics - both are in synaptics manager. They are easy to use once set up, and I think you can fix the problem there (I'm new too, so I'm not sure!)

---

### Post by teachop on 2007-04-30
I had that problem with my acer and added some lines to the "Synaptics Touchpad" section:


	Option		"MinSpeed"	"0.18"
	Option		"MaxSpeed"	"0.36"


You can get the instructions from "man synaptics".  There are different types of pads and the values for MinSpeed and MaxSpeed may be way off for you.  I just fiddled till I liked it.

---

### Post by cedron on 2007-05-01
Wow! I'm amazed by the generosity of knowledge of this forum. Two posts in a day, and now two problems solved. 

By installing gsynaptics and editing xorg.conf to the following:

 "InputDevice"        
        Identifier      "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver            "synaptics"
        Option          "SendCoreEvents"        "true"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/psaux"
        Option          "Protocol"              "auto-dev"
        Option          "HorizScrollDelta"      "0"
        Option          "SHMConfig"             "true"
        Option          "Minspeed"              "0.50"
        Option          "Maxspeed"              "1.4"

It worked like a charm. Thanks a lot for the reply!

---

### Post by teachop on 2007-05-01
I didn't install gsynaptics.  I just edited xorg.conf to set the pad speed up, which I guess isn't the most user friendly approach, but worked.

---

### Post by tcouto on 2007-05-07
followed this thread to increase the speed of my touchpad! worked pretty well, but i still had a hard time with double tapping (way too fast).. so... this settings worked like a charm:

Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Synaptics Touchpad"
Driver "synaptics"
Option "SendCoreEvents" "true"
Option "Device" "/dev/psaux"
Option "Protocol" "auto-dev"
Option "SHMConfig" "true"
Option "MaxTapTime" "300"
Option "MaxDoubleTapTime" "300"
Option "Minspeed" "1.00"
Option "Maxspeed" "1.00"
EndSection

hope it helps someone else!! 
thanks everyone

---
